description: A quantity of PEOPLE drawn as a crowd of tiny identical figures filling the frame, with a big headline number over it. The rare one can be singled out (circled/glowing/colored).
whenToUse: A population / how-many-people beat ('8 billion', '1.4 billion', '500 people', '1 in 10').
generalize: The crowd density, the number, and whether one figure is singled out are SLOTS. The number is the emphasis (often red).
produces: gpt-image-2 crowd of tiny figures as bgUrl; the big number = gpt-image-2 lettering (title-bold, red for magnitude) hard-appearing overlay; an optional 'one singled out' marker overlay.

crowd.note: SHORT description of the crowd (uniform vs diverse; density)

number.note: the headline number, e.g. '8 Billion' (title-bold, usually red)

singleOut.note: circle/mark one figure (for a '1 in N' ratio)

styleLock: Crowd = gpt-image-2 art; number = gpt-image-2 lettering (title-bold). White-bodied figures.
rules: Population is always tiny identical bodies.
The number hard-appears (title voice, red for magnitude).
For '1 in N', single out one figure.
